Class RewardSelectionGUI

java.lang.Object
net.sylphian.minecraft.crates.gui.RewardSelectionGUI
public class RewardSelectionGUI extends Object
GUI shown when a player must pick from multiple rolled rewards.

Displayed when CrateConfig.playerPicks() is less than CrateConfig.totalRolls(). Rolled rewards are shown as clickable items the player can toggle selected or deselected. Once the required number of rewards are selected, the player confirms via the bottom-row confirm button to receive their chosen rewards.

    Description
    static org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack
    asSelected(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack base)
    Returns a copy of the given item with a green ✔ prefix on its display name to indicate it has been selected by the player.

  • Method Details

    • open

      public static void open(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, List<RewardEntry> rewards, int picks, CrateService crateService)
      Opens the reward selection GUI for the given player.
      Parameters:
      player - the player choosing their rewards
      rewards - the rewards rolled from the crate
      picks - how many rewards the player may select
      crateService - the service used to build reward display items

    • buildConfirmButton

      public static org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack buildConfirmButton(int selected, int picks)
      Builds the confirm button item reflecting the current selection progress. The button uses a lime dye when at least one reward is selected and a gray dye when nothing is selected. The player may confirm with fewer than the maximum picks.
      Parameters:
      selected - the number of rewards currently selected
      picks - the total number of rewards the player must select
      Returns:
      the confirm button ItemStack

    • asSelected

      public static org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack asSelected(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack base)
      Returns a copy of the given item with a green ✔ prefix on its display name to indicate it has been selected by the player.
      Parameters:
      base - the original reward item to mark as selected
      Returns:
      a copy of the item with the selection indicator applied to its name
